senses.definition: a place in a plane, train, theater, etc. that is designed for people to sit on, particularly one requiring a ticket
a seat in a vehicle designed to accommodate passengers
a seat designed to provide support and comfort, often used in sports cars
a seat in a car, bus, or other vehicle where someone who is not driving can sit
the rear seating area of a vehicle, usually for passengers

examples.x: They installed a DVD player for entertainment in the back seat.
The back seat had integrated cup holders for convenience.
He adjusted the temperature controls for the back seat passengers.
She folded down the back seat to create more cargo space.
The children sat quietly in the back seat during the road trip.

senses.definition: the seat in a car that is next to the driver

examples.x: Sarah sat in the front seat while her dad drove the car.
Tom prefers the front seat because he gets less car sick there.
The front seat has more legroom than the back seats.
In our family, the front seat is reserved for adults only.
Jane adjusted the front seat to make herself more comfortable.

senses.definition: a seat used in a car to help small children sit higher and use the seatbelt properly

examples.x: When Ben turned four, his parents bought him a new booster seat for the car.
The law says children must use a booster seat until they are tall enough to use the regular seatbelt.
Lisa likes her booster seat because she can see out of the car windows better now.
It is important to check if the booster seat is installed correctly for safety.
Tom's dad helped him buckle up in his booster seat before they went on their trip.

senses.definition: a part of a car designed to keep a child's seat safely in place

examples.x: The child seat anchor helps to secure the baby seat firmly in the car.
Before driving, always check if the child seat anchor is correctly attached.
The new car has easy-to-use child seat anchors for added safety.
It is important to connect the seatbelt to the child seat anchor.
She made sure the child seat anchor was locked before starting the trip.

senses.definition: a seat or device in a car that keeps a child safe and secure while traveling

examples.x: Parents should always use a child restraint when driving with young children.
The new car seat is a type of child restraint designed for toddlers.
It is important to check that the child restraint is properly installed before starting the car.
The law requires a child restraint for children under a certain age or weight.
She made sure to buckle her baby into the child restraint before leaving for the trip.

senses.definition: a seat behind the main rider on a motorcycle

examples.x: She rode pillion on his motorcycle during the parade.
The pillion seat was equipped with grab handles for safety.
They added a cushioned pad to the pillion seat for long rides.
The passenger on the pillion wore a helmet for protection.
He adjusted the footrests for comfort while riding pillion.

senses.definition: the padded support for the head attached to the top of a vehicle seat

examples.x: The headrests in the back seats could be folded down for extra storage space.

senses.definition: a part of a chair or sofa that is designed to support the arms of a seated person, providing comfort and relaxation

examples.x: She rested her arms on the armrests while relaxing in the comfortable chair.
The armrest of the couch was covered in soft fabric for added comfort.
He adjusted the armrest to a more comfortable height before sitting down.
The recliner had padded armrests, making it perfect for long hours of sitting.
She noticed the armrest was broken and needed to be repaired.

senses.definition: a pedal in a vehicle that controls the speed by increasing or decreasing fuel intake

examples.x: She released the accelerator pedal to slow down for the turn.
The accelerator pedal was responsive to his touch.
He pressed the accelerator pedal gently to accelerate.
They replaced the worn-out accelerator pedal with a new one.
The accelerator pedal was designed for smooth operation.

senses.definition: the pedal that one uses to control the speed of a car, truck, etc. when it is moving

examples.x: He pressed the gas pedal to speed up on the highway.
She stepped on the gas pedal to merge onto the busy road.
The gas pedal felt smooth and responsive after the repair.
He quickly released the gas pedal to slow down before the stop sign.
The gas pedal got stuck, causing the car to accelerate uncontrollably.

senses.definition: a pedal in a manual transmission vehicle used to disengage and engage the engine from the gearbox

examples.x: He pressed the clutch pedal while shifting gears.
The clutch pedal required firm pressure to engage properly.
They installed a clutch pedal extension for easier reach.
The clutch pedal was essential for smooth gear changes.
She released the clutch pedal slowly to avoid stalling.

senses.definition: the pedal that one pushes with one's foot to stop or slow down a car, truck, etc.

examples.x: He pressed the brake pedal gently to slow down.
The brake pedal felt soft, indicating a potential issue.
She slammed the brake pedal to avoid hitting the car in front of her.
The brake pedal was stiff, making it harder to stop the car.
She accidentally stepped on the brake pedal while adjusting the seat.

senses.definition: a brake that is operated by hand to hold a vehicle in place

examples.x: He pulled the emergency brake to stop the car from rolling down the hill.
The driver engaged the emergency brake before exiting the parked car.
In case of brake failure, the emergency brake can be used to slow down the vehicle safely.
The mechanic adjusted the tension on the emergency brake cable to ensure it functions properly.
It's important to release the emergency brake before driving to avoid damaging the braking system.

senses.definition: a hand-operated brake used to keep a vehicle stationary when parked

examples.x: He engaged the parking brake before exiting the car.
They adjusted the tension of the parking brake cable.
She forgot to release the parking brake before driving off.
The parking brake lever was located next to the driver's seat.
The parking brake warning light blinked when engaged.

senses.definition: a handle in a car or other vehicle, by which a driver can change gears

examples.x: She shifted the gearshift into first gear as she started driving up the steep hill.
The car's manual transmission required frequent adjustments to the gearshift for smooth driving.
The gearshift on the truck was heavy, requiring a firm grip to change gears effectively.
She practiced using the gearshift in a parking lot to get comfortable with the manual transmission.
The mechanic repaired the faulty gearshift to ensure smooth gear transitions.

senses.definition: a gear in a vehicle's transmission used for moving backward

examples.x: He shifted into reverse gear to parallel park.
The reverse gear engaged smoothly without any noise.
She checked the rearview mirror before engaging reverse gear.
They replaced the reverse gear mechanism for better performance.
The reverse gear had a distinct sound when engaged.

senses.definition: the wheel that a driver holds or turns to make a vehicle move in different directions

examples.x: She adjusted the steering wheel to a comfortable position before starting her drive.
He turned the steering wheel sharply to avoid the obstacle in the road.
The car's steering wheel was equipped with controls for audio and cruise settings.
She noticed a problem with the steering wheel when it became difficult to turn while driving.
He gripped the steering wheel tightly as he navigated through the slippery conditions.

senses.definition: a part of a car that connects the steering wheel to the wheels, allowing the driver to control the direction of the car
a switch or button used to start and stop the vehicle's engine
the pedals and instruments in a car that allow both the driver and instructor to operate them simultaneously for learning purposes
a strap that keeps a person attached to their seat to prevent injuries, especially in cars, etc.
a safety device in a vehicle designed to inflate rapidly in the event of a collision
a small light on the dashboard that shows information about the car's systems, like fuel level, engine status, or warnings
a stick on the side of a car's steering wheel that is moved up or down to show other drivers the direction the driver intends to turn
a device placed inside of a vehicle that makes an alarming and loud sound, used to give a warning or signal to others
a metal framework in vehicles that prevents cabin collapse during rollover accidents
the panel placed inside a vehicle, below the glass at the front, that is facing the driver or pilot and contains most of the controls and switches
the area in front of the driver containing gauges, indicators, and controls
the instrument that shows the speed or mileage of the vehicle
a measuring instrument in a vehicle indicating the amount of fuel remaining in the tank
a round control used to adjust settings such as temperature, volume, or fan speed

examples.x: She turned the dial to increase the temperature in the car.
He adjusted the dial on the radio to find his favorite station.
The dial for adjusting the air conditioning was easy to read.
They installed a new dial for the car's climate control system.
The dial for the headlights allowed him to switch between high and low beams.

senses.definition: a device in a vehicle that measures the distance traveled by counting the number of rotations of a vehicle's wheels

examples.x: The odometer on the car showed that it had traveled over 100,000 miles.
He checked the odometer regularly to track the mileage for maintenance purposes.
The odometer reading indicated that they were nearing their destination.
The mechanic replaced the odometer in the truck to ensure accurate mileage recording.
The odometer reset to zero after the vehicle's battery was replaced.

senses.definition: a measuring instrument displaying the revolutions per minute of an engine

examples.x: He monitored the tachometer to shift gears at optimal revolutions per minute.
She noticed the tachometer needle spike during acceleration.
The tachometer reading indicated a smooth engine performance.
They installed a digital tachometer for precise revolutions per minute measurement.
The tachometer was color-coded for easy revolutions per minute range identification.

senses.definition: a device that shows how hot or cold something is

examples.x: The car's temperature gauge showed that the engine was getting too hot.
She checked the temperature gauge on the oven to make sure it was at the right setting.
The temperature gauge in the room indicated it was 25 degrees Celsius.
He installed a new temperature gauge in the fish tank to monitor the water.
The weather station uses a temperature gauge to measure the outside temperature.

senses.definition: a device in a car that shows the pressure of the oil in the engine

examples.x: The oil pressure gauge in my car shows if the engine has enough oil pressure.
When the oil pressure gauge drops to zero, it means there might be a problem with the engine.
It is important to check the oil pressure gauge regularly to avoid engine damage.
If the oil pressure gauge is not working, you should get it fixed as soon as possible.
The mechanic explained how to read the oil pressure gauge and what the different levels mean.

senses.definition: the storage area and control panel located between the driver's and front passenger's seats in a car

examples.x: I put my phone in the center console so it doesn't slide around while I'm driving.
The center console has a small compartment where I keep my sunglasses.
She adjusted the air conditioning settings using the buttons on the center console.
The new car has a large center console with cup holders and extra storage space.
He plugged his charger into the outlet in the center console to charge his phone.

senses.definition: a small section in a vehicle's dashboard for storing small items

examples.x: He found a forgotten pair of sunglasses in the glove compartment.
The glove compartment door closed securely with a click.
They added a divider to organize the glove compartment contents.
The glove compartment light turned on automatically when opened.
She kept her registration and insurance documents in the glove compartment.

senses.definition: a small, built-in container in a car or other vehicle that holds a cup or bottle to prevent it from spilling

examples.x: I always put my coffee in the cup holder when I drive to work.
The cup holder in my car can fit both small and large bottles.
She reached for her water bottle in the cup holder while waiting at the traffic light.
The new car model has a cup holder for every passenger seat.
My friend spilled his drink because his car doesn't have a cup holder.

senses.definition: the amount of space available for a person's legs when sitting, especially in vehicles or seats

examples.x: The economy class seats on the plane had limited legroom, so my knees were pressed against the seat in front of me.
He chose the aisle seat because it usually offers more legroom than the window seat.
In the new car model, they increased the legroom in the back seats to make it more comfortable for passengers.
The theater had spacious seating with ample legroom, which made the long performance more enjoyable.
We had to book tickets early to ensure we got seats with good legroom for the long bus journey.

senses.definition: a hinged flap above the windshield in a vehicle to shade the driver from sunlight

examples.x: She pulled down the sun visor to shield her eyes from the glare of the sun.
He found a parking ticket tucked behind the sun visor.
The sun visor had a mirror on its underside for checking his appearance.
They replaced the sun visors with ones that had built-in lights.
The sun visor could be adjusted to block sunlight from different angles.

senses.definition: a fixed mirror inside of a car or another vehicle that gives the driver a view of what is happening behind the vehicle

examples.x: He adjusted the rearview mirror to get a better view of the traffic behind him.
The rearview mirror was cracked after the accident.
She checked the rearview mirror before changing lanes.
He noticed a car in the rearview mirror speeding up.
He cleaned the rearview mirror before starting his drive.

senses.definition: a type of camera that is mounted on the dashboard or windshield of a vehicle to record video footage of the road ahead or inside the car
